title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after performing four consecutive nights at Fenway Park.
Kahan joins Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and the Zac Brown Band as one of only four artists in the Hall of Fame.
Kahan's historic Fenway run featured fireworks, a surprise announcement by his mother, and cameo appearances from Boston sports figures.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from Cash App's $45 million settlement over alleged deceptive practices.
Block Inc., which owns Cash App, allegedly misled consumers about app safety and failed to protect them from fraud.
The settlement mandates improved fraud protection, truthful safety claims, and increased customer support for Cash App users.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly took a police boat onto Lake Mascuppic, activating its emergency lights.
Authorities report the man attempted to enforce local boating rules, ordering people to leave the lake.
Tyngsborough police plan to summon the man for a court appearance on charges of impersonating a police officer.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: More than 60 international tall ships and military vessels paraded into Boston Harbor for the Sail Boston 250 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail, representing over 20 countries, docking at various waterfront locations in Boston.
Thousands of visitors attended Sail Boston 250, celebrating Boston's maritime history as the final stop of the Sail250 tour.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox drafted Jake Schaffner, UNC shortstop, as No. 20 overall pick in 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner led UNC in runs with 85, hitting .356 with six homers and 50 RBI in 68 games.
MLB.com rated Schaffner 75th prospect; noted for speed and defense, but arm strength may shift him to second base.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston Grand Parade of Sail showcases historic ships, led by USS Constitution, offering a unique generational tribute.
Spectators gathered from Castle Island to Piers Park, celebrating the 250th anniversary with ships including the Bluenose Two and Spirit of Bermuda.
Sail Boston serves as a significant cultural event, enhancing Boston's international profile alongside events like the World Cup.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Athletic named Brayan Bello the midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner for poor performance despite the Red Sox's recent success.
Bello had a 10.35 ERA in eight starts, with a .365 opponent batting average and 10 home runs allowed before being demoted.
In relief, Bello excelled with a 0.71 ERA and 22 strikeouts over 25.1 innings, but remains in Triple-A Worcester.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox are closing in on an AL wild-card spot with a seven-game winning streak.
Uncertainty looms as Boston debates whether to buy talent or sell assets before the trade deadline.
GM Craig Breslow faces pressure to choose a firm direction for the team at the 2026 deadline.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden on July 10, 2026, as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
Her latest album, "Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran," won the 2025 Grammy for Best Latin Pop Album after a seven-year music hiatus.
The show featured a mix of nostalgic hits and new songs, with elaborate stage elements and dynamic dance performances.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Healey announced $18.6 million in tax credits to build 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
The funding supports projects converting historic buildings and underused properties in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
The tax credits aim to address high housing costs and shortages, with $1.5 million allocated to convert Fall River's Durfee Block.
